chapter_title.ar: باب الِاسْتِبْرَاءِ مِنَ الْبَوْلِ وَ غَسْلِهِ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَجِدِ الْمَاءَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The Istibra’a from the urine, and washing it, and the one who cannot find the water

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/62)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/62)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ بَالَ ثُمَّ تَوَضَّأَ وَ قَامَ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ فَوَجَدَ بَلَلًا قَالَ لَا يَتَوَضَّأُ إِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحَبَائِلِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Abu Dawood, altogether from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Al A’ala, from Ibn Abu Yafour who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about a man who urinates, then he performs Ablution (Wudhu)s and stands for the Prayer, so he finds wetness. Heasws said: ‘He would not perform Ablution (Wudhu). But rather, that is from the sexual veins’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from and abu Dawud all al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Safwan ibn Yahya from al-‘Ala’ ibn abu Ya‘fur who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who urinates, then takes Wudu 5 . He then stands up for Salah (prayer) and finds a certain moisture. He (the Imam) said, ’He does not need Wudu 5 ; it is of Haba’il (passageways, veins).”
